title: Applied AI & Partnerships
description:
Applied AI & Partnerships

Applied & Partnerships

SFO or BOS

Full-time

On-site

The Role

We are looking for an exceptional individual to join our Applied AI & Partnerships team in a high-impact, high-ownership role. This role is critical to the company: you will help build and scale our ecosystem by driving partner activation and growth, shape the ecosystem strategy that expands adoption, and ensure partner learnings (qualitative and quantitative) flow back into product and model improvements.

You will be a central force in building and advancing relationships, maximizing their utility for both the company and our partners, and keeping complex, cross-functional work moving. Being optimally successful requires building a deep understanding of what matters to partners, earning their trust through responsiveness and follow-through, and aligning roadmaps and expectations across teams. You should be comfortable engaging with a diversity of partners and industries, and flexing between strategic thinking, operational rigor, and crisp external and internal communication.

This is a high-growth role with significant visibility, direct exposure to leadership, and real ownership of outcomes. This role is in-person five days a week based in San Mateo, CA.

Responsibilities

Strategic partnership execution: Own and drive a portfolio of high-impact partner initiatives end-to-end, ensuring alignment with company goals and maximizing long-term value.

Partnership evaluation and selection: Evaluate incoming ecosystem and partnership opportunities, prioritize them, and ensure alignment with strategic objectives.

Ecosystem growth strategy: Define and drive ecosystem initiatives that expand adoption and deepen partner engagement (programs, partner journeys, scalable motions, playbooks).

Executive communication: Serve as a primary point of contact for partner workstreams—respond rapidly, navigate complex organizational structures, and keep initiatives moving forward through clear next steps and timelines.

Cross-functional coordination: Centralize communication and documentation across product, ML/engineering, GTM, and partnerships stakeholders to optimize efficiency and minimize disruption.

Operational execution: Translate ambiguity into execution, coordinate the details, and follow through relentlessly

Communications and narrative: Develop and ship ecosystem-facing and internal materials to ensure consistent messaging around launches and key moments.

Metrics and tracking: Develop and maintain centralized tracking systems to monitor ecosystem/partnership metrics, milestones, and outcomes; use data to drive decisions and next actions.

Insights and feedback loops: Capture partner learnings and package them into actionable internal recommendations supporting durable product/model feedback loops.

Travel: Ability to travel periodically to build relationships, support partners, and attend conferences as needed.

Minimum Qualifications

3–5 years of professional experience in a highly demanding, high-ownership environment, such as management consulting, technology companies, and/or startups, with a track record of delivering high-quality work on tight timelines.

Proven experience driving cross-functional initiatives in one or more of: strategy & ops/biz ops, partnerships, ecosystem programs, product ops, program management, or growth/ops.

Demonstrated ability to create clarity from ambiguity, prioritize effectively, and execute end-to-end with minimal oversight.

Exceptional written and verbal communication skills; able to produce crisp partner-facing narratives and exec-ready internal updates.

Strong interpersonal skills and high responsiveness; able to build trust with external partners and internal teams at all levels.

Comfort with metrics and operational tracking; able to build and maintain simple systems (trackers, reporting cadences, templates) and use data to drive decisions.

High integrity and reliability; consistent follow-through and strong ownership mindset.

Preferred Qualifications

Prior experience building or scaling ecosystem motions, such as in a growth-stage startup.

Experience working in AI/ML, robotics, developer platforms, or other complex technical/product-driven ecosystems.

Experience owning partner/customer engagements from kickoff through launch and ongoing success.

Familiarity with product management fundamentals (requirements, success metrics, prioritization, roadmap inputs) and translating external feedback into internal action.

Demonstrated ability to craft clear external narratives and crisp internal decision memos for high-visibility workstreams.

What we offer

Real ownership and real outcomes: you’ll own meaningful, externally-facing workstreams that directly impact ecosystem growth, partner success, and what we build next.

High-trust, high-autonomy environment: we move fast with a bias toward action. You’ll have latitude to define the approach, build lightweight systems, and make decisions to keep momentum.

Direct exposure and visibility: you’ll work closely with senior leaders and key cross-functional stakeholders, with frequent opportunities to shape priorities, narratives, and execution.

A rare mix of strategy and execution: this role sits at the intersection of ecosystem strategy, partner delivery, communications/narrative, and operating cadence—ideal if you like both “figuring it out” and “making it real.”

Steep learning curve and career acceleration: you’ll build durable skills in partnership strategy, program design, stakeholder management, product/ML feedback loops, and executive communication.

Room to build: you’ll create playbooks, templates, and programs that scale—your work becomes the operating system others rely on.

Collaborative, mission-driven team: a world-class group that values rigor, candor, and follow-through, and cares deeply about building responsibly and effectively.

title: Chief of Staff to the CEO
description:
Chief of Staff to the CEO

People

SFO Only

Full-time

On-site

The Role

We are looking for an exceptional individual to serve as Chief of Staff to the CEO, Pete Florence.

https://www.linkedin.com/in/peteflorence/

https://x.com/peteflorence

This role is high-impact, high-ownership, and critical to the company: you will act as a force multiplier for the CEO, ensuring that priorities are clear, execution is tight, and nothing important falls through the cracks.

You will operate at the center of the company, connecting product, research, operations, and external stakeholders, while driving forward the highest-leverage initiatives. This role requires strong judgment, high agency, and the ability to translate ambiguity into execution.

You will help design and run the CEO’s operating system: maintaining context across everything in flight, preparing for key decisions and meetings, and ensuring consistent follow-through across the organization. You should be comfortable flexing between strategy and execution, handling both high-level prioritization and detailed operational work with equal rigor.

This is a high-growth role with significant visibility, direct exposure to leadership, and real ownership of outcomes.

This role is in-person five days a week based in San Mateo, CA.

Responsibilities

Executive leverage and prioritization: Maintain a real-time understanding of company priorities, ensure the CEO is focused on the highest-leverage work, and proactively surface risks, tradeoffs, and opportunities.

Cross-functional execution: Drive high-priority, cross-functional initiatives end-to-end, including coordinating across partnerships, research and engineering, and operations to ensure progress and alignment.

Special projects: Lead ambiguous, high-impact projects (e.g., fundraising support, partnerships, internal initiatives) from zero to one, with full ownership of outcomes.

Systems and process building: Develop lightweight systems, trackers, and workflows that improve visibility, accountability, and execution across the company.

Information filtering and triage: Act as a central point for inbound information and requests; filter signal from noise, prioritize effectively, and ensure timely responses.

Meeting and stakeholder management: Prepare the CEO for key meetings, track decisions, and execute on action items. Maintain context across key relationships, ensure thoughtful follow-ups, and support high-stakes communications.

Minimum Qualifications

2-5 years of professional experience in a highly demanding, high-ownership environment, such as startups, technology companies, consulting, or venture capital, with a track record of delivering high-quality work on tight timelines.

Experience driving cross-functional initiatives in one or more of: strategy & operations, business operations, product operations, program management, or founder/CEO support roles.

Demonstrated ability to create clarity from ambiguity, prioritize effectively, and execute end-to-end with minimal oversight.

Exceptional written and verbal communication skills; able to produce crisp, executive-ready materials and synthesize complex information clearly.

Strong judgment and decision-making ability; able to identify what matters most and act accordingly.

High agency and ownership mindset; consistently proactive with strong follow-through.

Strong interpersonal skills and high responsiveness; able to build trust with senior stakeholders and operate effectively across teams.

Comfort building simple systems (trackers, processes, cadences) and using them to drive execution and accountability.

Excitement and energy to work in robotics and at the frontier of intelligence.

Preferred Qualifications

Prior experience working directly with a founder, CEO, or senior executive in a high-growth environment.

Experience in AI/ML, robotics, or other technical/product-driven companies.

Experience supporting or participating in fundraising, partnerships, or other high-stakes external processes.

Familiarity with product development processes and the ability to translate strategic priorities into execution.

Demonstrated ability to operate across both strategic and operational domains, with a bias toward action.

What we offer

Real ownership and real outcomes: You will operate at the center of the company and directly impact how the CEO and leadership team function and execute.

High-trust, high-autonomy environment: We move quickly and expect strong judgment; you will have significant latitude to define priorities, systems, and approaches.

Direct exposure and visibility:You will work closely with the CEO and senior leaders, with meaningful influence over company priorities and decisions.

A rare mix of strategy and execution: This role spans prioritization, operations, communication, and execution—ideal if you enjoy both thinking and doing.

Steep learning curve and career acceleration: You will develop deep skills in company building, executive decision-making, and operating at scale.

Room to build: You will design and implement the systems and processes that become the company’s operating backbone.

Collaborative, mission-driven team: A world-class group that values rigor, ownership, and speed, and is deeply committed to building transformative technology.

title: Creative Producer
description:
Creative Producer

Brand & Communications

SFO or BOS

Full-time

On-site

About the role:

We’re hiring an in-house Creative Producer to help bring our robotics company to life through video. This person will own the end-to-end creation of video content: filming, editing, and organizing our media library so great footage is easy to find and use. You’ll work closely with founders and the team to capture product demos, milestones, behind-the-scenes moments, recruiting content, and brand storytelling. You should be excited to operate in a fast-moving startup environment and comfortable turning technical work into clear, compelling content.

This is a hands-on role for someone who is strong in videography, editing, and production organization. Experience supporting or lightly managing social media accounts is a plus, but the core of the role is making excellent video content and building a clean, reliable content workflow.

Responsibilities:

Plan, shoot, and edit video content for product marketing, launches, recruiting, social media, and company storytelling

Capture robots, demos, prototypes, team interviews, events, and day-to-day moments in a visually compelling way

Edit short-form and long-form videos for different channels and formats

Own post-production workflows including selects, project files, exports, versioning, and archiving

Organize and maintain our video library so footage is searchable, accessible, and easy for the team to reuse

Create systems for file naming, folder structure, asset tagging, and backup management

Collaborate with founders, engineers, and other teammates to identify and produce high-value stories

Help translate technical work into content that is clear and engaging for external audiences

Support publishing and management of social media content as needed

Contribute creative ideas for how we show the technical capabilities, products, team, and company to the world

Qualifications:

3+ years of experience in videography, video editing, or content production

Strong shooting skills with an eye for composition, lighting, pacing, and storytelling

Strong editing skills and good creative judgment

Excellent file organization and media management habits, you have library science nerdiness inside you

Ability to independently manage projects from concept through final delivery

Comfort working in a startup environment with speed, ambiguity, and changing priorities

Strong communicator who can work cross-functionally with technical and non-technical teammates

Willingness to be scrappy and hands-on

Professional experience with video cameras and production equipment

Strong proficiency in common editing software such as Final Cut Pro, Adobe Suite, and/or similar tools

Experience managing footage, project files, and delivery assets across shared drives or team systems

Nice to have:

Experience creating content for startups, hardware companies, robotics companies, or technical products

Experience producing content for recruiting or employer brand

Familiarity with social media publishing workflows and channel best practices

Basic photography, motion graphics, or sound editing skills

Experience building lightweight DAM or content archive systems

Interest in robotics, engineering, or emerging technology

Why this role matters:

We’re building in robotics, and a huge part of our story is visual. The work, the product, the demos, the people, and the progress all come alive on camera. This role will shape how customers, candidates, and the broader world understand what we’re building.

You’ll help us create the footage and systems that make our content engine work — not just one-off videos, but a repeatable way to capture and tell our story well.

title: Engineering Technician
description:
Engineering Technician

Operations

SFO or BOS

Full-time

On-site

About the Role

This is a hands-on, execution-driven role at the center of building our robots.

As an Engineering Technician, you’ll be responsible for turning parts into working systems — quickly, precisely, and reliably. You’ll help ensure builds are clean, repeatable, and scalable.

In practice, you’ll assemble mechanical systems, perform basic electrical work (wiring, connectors, simple integrations), run checks, catch issues early, and help improve how we build. If something is off — misaligned parts, loose connections, unclear instructions, inefficiencies in the process — you fix it or flag it.

This role is about making sure our robots come together correctly, every time.

You’ll be responsible for:

Assembling mechanical components into fully functioning robotic systems

Performing basic electrical work (routing wires, connecting components, installing connectors)

Reading and executing build instructions with high accuracy

Using hand tools, power tools, and specialized equipment to perform precise assembly work

Inspecting and testing assemblies to ensure quality and functionality

Identifying defects, misalignments, or inconsistencies and escalating quickly

Following SOPs, safety standards, and best practices during all builds

Maintaining a clean, organized, and efficient workspace

Collaborating with engineers and teammates to hit build timelines

Contributing to process improvements to make builds faster and more reliable

You might thrive in this role if you:

Have hands-on experience with mechanical assembly or hardware builds

Have some exposure to basic electrical work (wiring, connectors, harnesses)

Are detail-oriented and take pride in clean, precise work

Are comfortable using tools and working with physical systems

Catch problems early and don’t ignore small issues

Like working with your hands and seeing tangible results

Can follow structured processes but also improve them

Are reliable, consistent, and move with urgency

Enjoy being part of a fast-moving, build-heavy environment

Like being the person who makes things actually come together

What This Role Is Not

This is not a purely theoretical or design-focused role

This is not a robotics software or controls engineering position

This is not a deep electrical engineering role

This is not a passive “follow instructions only” job

Instead, this role sits on the front lines of building real robotic systems — where mechanical precision and clean electrical work directly impact what ships.

Growth & Impact

As we scale, this role grows with the complexity and volume of our builds.

You’ll have the opportunity to take on more ownership across build processes, quality systems, and production workflows — helping shape how our robots are assembled at scale.

If you want to work hands-on with cutting-edge robotics and be directly responsible for bringing systems to life, this role has real impact.

title: Member of Talent Staff
description:
Member of Talent Staff

People

SFO or BOS

Full-time

On-site

About the Role

We’re looking for Generalist’s first full-time member of the talent team. This is a full-cycle, own-everything, multi-disciplinary role to shape the trajectory of this highly talent dense team. The primary focus is full-cycle technical recruiting; however, as the founding member, there will also be needs for other kinds of roles as well.

You’ll be responsible for:

Working directly with the founders and executive leadership on hiring key roles across the company

Owning the full recruiting cycle from sourcing to closing

Shaping appropriate processes and resources required to run an efficient hiring process

You might thrive in this role if you:

Have extensive experience doing full-cycle recruiting

Show an exceptional ability to get up to speed quickly on deeply technical roles

Have a remarkable intuition for people and the ability to read between the lines

Have a record of adapting processes to suit the current needs of the team and business

We use Ashby as our ATS.

title: Office Manager
description:
Office Manager

Operations

SFO Only

Full-time

On-site

About the Role

This is a deeply cross-functional, execution-oriented role at the center of our workplace operations.

As an Office Manager, you will ensure that the physical office, vendors, systems, and employee experience all come together into a high-functioning, high-velocity environment. You won’t sit purely in admin, facilities, HR, or events — you will operate horizontally across all of them.

In practice, you will run the day-to-day office, coordinate vendors and services, manage budgets, support onboarding, and design the systems that keep everything running smoothly. You may rearrange physical spaces, troubleshoot infrastructure issues, negotiate contracts, and build repeatable processes that scale as the company grows.

This role is about making the entire workplace operate seamlessly. If something is slowing the team down — whether it’s missing supplies, broken equipment, unclear processes, vendor issues, or gaps in employee experience — you fix it, or find the right person to fix it.

You are the connective tissue between people, space, and operations.

You’ll be responsible for:

Running daily office operations and ensuring everything functions smoothly

Designing and improving systems for office processes (supplies, vendors, events, onboarding)

Managing vendors (cleaning, internet, food, maintenance) and negotiating contracts

Coordinating deliveries, services, and ongoing facility needs

Owning office budgets, tracking spend, and processing invoices/reimbursements

Ensuring the office space is organized, well-maintained, and optimized for team productivity

Supporting employee onboarding (workspace setup, access, equipment readiness)

Planning and executing team events, lunches, and offsites

Managing shared spaces (conference rooms, calendars, common areas)

Handling mail, shipping, and general administrative workflows

Identifying operational bottlenecks and improving the office experience end-to-end

You might thrive in this role if you:

Have experience running office operations, workplace experience, or facilities in a fast-paced environment

Are highly organized and can manage multiple moving parts simultaneously

Think in systems and processes, not one-off fixes

Are comfortable coordinating across vendors, employees, and leadership

Have strong ownership and proactively solve problems without being asked

Enjoy both physical and operational work (moving things, setting up spaces, fixing issues)

Have high emotional intelligence and care about team experience and culture

Write clear documentation and create repeatable workflows

Prefer execution and speed over over-planning

Like being the person who “just makes it work”

What This Role Is Not

This role works closely with leadership, HR, and operations. However:

This is not an Executive Assistant role focused on calendar or travel management

This is not a purely administrative role with narrowly defined tasks

This is not a facilities-only role limited to maintenance and repairs

This is not an events-only role focused solely on culture programming

Instead, this role sits at the intersection of operations, workplace experience, and execution — ensuring the office runs end-to-end and continuously improves.

If you are most excited by ownership, building systems, and making environments run seamlessly — this role may be a strong fit.

Growth & Impact

This role sits on the front lines of scaling our workplace as the company grows as the team expands, the scope evolves from managing a single office to shaping workplace strategy, office expansion, vendor ecosystems, and employee experience at scale.

Career growth here is directly tied to the scale and complexity of the company. The more you can operationalize — across systems, budgets, vendors, and culture — the more ownership and influence you will earn.

If you want to grow alongside a fast-scaling AI + robotics company and play a critical role in how teams operate day-to-day, this role offers outsized impact.

Office Manager

Operations

Full-time

On-site

This is a deeply cross-functional, execution-oriented role at the center of our workplace operations.

title: Research Scientist: Post-Training
description:
Research Scientist: Post-Training

Technical Staff

SFO or BOS

Full-time

On-site

About the Role

Pretraining gives us a general model. Post-training makes it useful, controllable, safe, and performant in the real world. You will train large pretrained robot models into production-ready systems via fine-tuning, reinforcement learning, steering, human feedback, task specialization, evaluation, and on-robot validation—at scale. Regardless of your initial background, you will grow into becoming a full-stack ML roboticist capable of quickly pinpoint issues on either side of ML or controls, and all the places in between. This is where research meets reality.

You’ll be responsible for:

Designing fine-tuning and adaptation strategies for downstream robotic tasks and embodiments

Developing methods for improving reliability, robustness, and controllability

Building evaluation frameworks that measure real-world robot performance, not just offline metrics

Improving inference-time performance (latency, stability, memory footprint) in collaboration with ML infrastructure

Leveraging techniques such as imitation learning, RL, distillation, synthetic data, and curriculum learning

Closing the loop between model outputs and physical-world outcomes

You might thrive in this role if you:

Have experience with fine-tuning large models for downstream tasks (RLHF, IL, RL, distillation, domain adaptation, etc.)

Have worked on embodied AI, robotics, or real-world ML systems

Care deeply about evaluation, benchmarking, and failure analysis

Are comfortable debugging across the ML stack — from loss curves to robot behavior

Enjoy rapid iteration with real-world feedback loops

Want to bridge the gap between foundation models and physical deployment

title: Research Scientist: Pretraining
description:
Research Scientist: Pretraining

Technical Staff

SFO or BOS

Full-time

On-site

About the Role

You will build the base intelligence layer for robotics. We train large-scale robot foundation models from massive multimodal datasets spanning video, proprioception, action traces, language, and more. You will design and run the core large-scale training efforts that give our models fundamentally new general capabilities across embodiments, tasks, and environments. You will “live and breathe” all forms of robot data.

You’ll be responsible for:

Designing and executing large-scale pretraining runs for robot foundation models (transformer- and diffusion-based architectures)

Defining model architectures, objectives, and training curricula across multimodal robotic data (vision, action, state, language)

Developing scalable data mixtures and sampling strategies across petabyte-scale datasets

Guiding data collection operations towards new directions, as well as sourcing new datasets

Running ablations to understand scaling laws, data quality effects, and architecture tradeoffs

Collaborating closely with ML Infra and Systems to push cluster utilization, throughput, and reliability

Turning raw robotic interaction data into generalizable model capabilities

You might thrive in this role if you:

Have deep experience training large transformer or diffusion models at scale (for generative models e.g. including language models, audio models, or video models)

Have led or significantly contributed to multi-node, multi-GPU distributed training efforts

Have worked on scaling laws, optimization dynamics, and large-model failure modes

Have strong PyTorch fundamentals and comfort debugging at every layer of the stack

Care about both empirical rigor and raw iteration speed

Are excited about building general-purpose robot intelligence from first principles

title: Robot Learning Generalist
description:
Robot Learning Generalist

Technical Staff

SFO or BOS

Full-time

On-site

About the Role:

This is a deeply cross-functional, execution-oriented role at the center of our robotics stack.

As an Robot Learning Generalist, you will ensure that robots, data, models, and evaluations all come together into a tight, high-velocity feedback loop. You won’t sit purely in ML research, robotics controls, hardware, or operations — you will operate horizontally across all of them.

In practice, you will help design tasks, coordinate robot data collection, kick off training jobs, run evaluations, analyze results, and ensure robots are physically ready for rollouts. You may build physical benchmarks, lightly modify hardware setups, test third-party tooling, and write documentation that enables others to replicate and scale your work.

This role is about making the entire system move faster. If something is blocking model progress — whether it’s missing data, unclear task definitions, misconfigured hardware, broken eval scripts, or unclear documentation — you fix it, or find the right person to fix it.

You are the connective tissue between research, infrastructure, hardware, and field operations.

You’ll be responsible for:

Designing new robotic tasks and benchmarks to evaluate model capabilities

Procuring materials and building lightweight physical benchmarks (e.g., Montessori task boards, manipulation setups)

Coordinating robot data collection efforts across internal operators and partners

Ensuring robots are properly configured, calibrated, and ready for rollouts and evaluations

Launching model training jobs and tracking experiments across multiple variants

Running structured evaluations and measuring real-world success rates

Analyzing results and closing feedback loops with ML researchers

Beta testing internal and third-party tools for teaching robots new skills

Writing clear documentation and playbooks so others can reproduce workflows

Identifying operational bottlenecks and improving system throughput end-to-end

You might thrive in this role if you:

Have hands-on experience with robot data collection, evaluation, or deployment

Have trained or fine-tuned ML models and understand the full lifecycle from data → training → evaluation

Are comfortable running experiments and tracking real-world metrics across multiple model variants

Enjoy operating across software, hardware, and physical systems

Have some exposure to basic EE/ME tasks (wiring, mounting sensors, assembling fixtures, debugging hardware)

Are highly organized and can coordinate multiple moving parts simultaneously

Write clear, structured documentation

Prefer execution and iteration speed over theoretical purity

Like being the person who “just makes it work”

What This Role Is Not

You will be a part of the ML team, and working very closely with ML, brainstorming ideas, and may prototype as well. However:

This is not a pure ML research role focused on designing new model architectures or advancing core learning algorithms.

This is not a large-scale infrastructure engineering role building distributed systems, databases, or UI platforms.

This is not a deep robotics controls or firmware engineering role.

Instead, this role sits at the intersection of ML, robotics, and operations — ensuring our systems run end-to-end in the real world, and improving them through tight execution loops.

If you are most excited by hands-on iteration, cross-functional execution, and accelerating the entire system — this role may be a strong fit.

Growth & Impact

This role sits on the front lines of scaling our robot fleet — both for internal R&D and external product deployments. As our robots expand in number, capability, and customer exposure, the complexity and leverage of this role grows with it.

Career growth here is directly tied to the scale and impact of our robots in the real world. The more surface area you can reliably operationalize — across data collection, training loops, evaluation rigor, and deployment readiness — the more influence and ownership you will earn.

If you want to grow alongside a rapidly scaling embodied AI system and shape how robots move from research to widespread deployment and applications, this role offers unusually high leverage.

title: Robot Operator
description:
Robot Operator

Operations

SFO Only

Full-time

On-site

About the Role

This is a hands-on, execution-driven role at the front lines of deploying and operating our robots in the real world.

As a Robot Operator, you’ll be responsible for keeping robots running reliably, safely, and at high performance. You won’t just monitor systems — you’ll actively operate, troubleshoot, and improve them.

In practice, you’ll deploy robots, diagnose issues in real time, and work closely with engineering to resolve problems and improve system performance. If something breaks, behaves unexpectedly, or slows operations down — you step in, figure it out, and keep things moving.

This role is about making sure our robots actually work.

You’ll be responsible for:

Deploying, powering, and calibrating robots for daily operations

Monitoring robot performance and system health in real time

Troubleshooting issues quickly and escalating when needed

Performing routine maintenance, firmware updates, and sensor calibrations

Logging operational data, issues, and performance metrics

Ensuring robots operate safely and within defined protocols

Working closely with engineers to validate fixes and test improvements

Supporting multi-robot operations and keeping fleets running smoothly

Identifying recurring issues and helping improve reliability over time

You might thrive in this role if you:

Come from a hands-on background (mechanical, electrical, automotive, HVAC, fabrication, or similar)

Have experience operating or maintaining complex equipment in the field

Are comfortable troubleshooting mechanical or electrical systems

Stay calm under pressure and solve problems quickly

Are comfortable using software tools, dashboards, or control interfaces

Take pride in clean, safe, and reliable work

Like working on real systems, not just theory

Are proactive, accountable, and don’t wait to be told what to fix

Enjoy being the person who keeps things running

Are excited about stepping into robotics and learning fast

Bonus if you:

Have worked with multi-machine or fleet-style operations

Have experience commissioning or starting up equipment

Have exposure to automation systems (PLCs, CNCs, sensors, motor controls, etc.)

Have worked with new or evolving systems where processes aren’t fully defined

Have documented issues, repairs, or maintenance in structured ways

What This Role Is Not

This is not a purely monitoring or passive operations role

This is not a robotics software or research position

This is not a role where you escalate everything and wait

Instead, this role sits at the intersection of hardware, operations, and execution — where you are expected to act, troubleshoot, and keep systems running in real time.

Growth & Impact

As our fleet grows, your impact grows with it — from operating a few systems to helping define how we run large-scale robotic operations reliably.

You’ll have opportunities to take on more ownership across deployments, system reliability, and operational processes — directly shaping how our robots perform in the real world.

If you want to work hands-on with cutting-edge robotics and be the person who keeps it all running, this role has real leverage.

title: Software Engineer: Applications
description:
Software Engineer: Applications

Technical Staff

SFO or BOS

Full-time

On-site

About the Role

You will build the applications that customers, deployment partners, researchers, and other internal teams will use to diagnose, teach, QA, and run our robots.

This is modern full-stack web and API engineering in an AI-augmented world.

You’ll be responsible for:

Crafting external and internal API interfaces that customers and partners interface with to access Generalist systems

Building the tools and workflows that enable and accelerate the core missions of researchers, engineers, and operations partners company-wide.

The primary web application researchers, ops, and deployments use to evaluate and train robot models

You might thrive in this role if you:

Have strong intuitions for what makes for great user interfaces, and user experiences

Understand modern web stack technologies: Typescript, React, FastAPI, Postgres, AWS

Understand how to expertly manage and leverage modern AI coding agents to accelerate development while maintaining high quality production standards

title: Software Engineer: Infrastructure
description:
Software Engineer: Infrastructure

Technical Staff

SFO or BOS

Full-time

On-site

About the Role

This role broadly owns infrastructure across the stack. If it’s running in the cloud, you probably care about it. The life blood of robotics is data, and we need high scale infrastructure to manage, store, and process petabytes of this data. Our APIs, services, and monitoring systems depend on the infrastructure you’ll be managing.

You’ll be responsible for:

The compute (both CPU and GPU compute) that’s required to cost effectively process petabytes of data at high throughput

The infrastructure required to process and store petabytes of data

Maintaining the security posture of our infrastructure and data

You might thrive in this role if you:

Have at least 5 years of experience managing high scale cloud infrastructure via tools like Kubernetes, and Terraform. Our services are predominantly in Python

Strong familiarity with AWS services (or their cloud equivalents), and their permission models

Strong opinions on how to properly use coding agents in an infrastructure setting

title: Software Engineer: ML Infra
description:
Software Engineer: ML Infra

Technical Staff

SFO or BOS

Full-time

On-site

About the Role

Generalist trains very large robot foundation models. This requires utilizing very large numbers of the latest generation GPU hardware and infrastructure (currently Nvidia) to run distributed training jobs and researcher experiments. We have extreme requirements on storage and data loading infrastructure that requires maximizing cloud infrastructure and custom solutions.

You will also own inference infrastructure. For our robots this is a fleet of on-prem GPUs attached to robots that have extreme real-time and latency budgets in compute constrained environments.

You’ll be responsible for:

Owning our GPU compute fleets

Ensure our GPUs are easy for researchers to use and maximally utilized

Optimizing and improving ML data loading transport and storage in highly distributed fully utilized environments.

Orchestration of robot inference fleets

You might thrive in this role if you:

Have managed large fleets of GPUs doing large-scale, long-term, highly distributed training runs or inference

Deep experience in Slurm or Kubernetes for ML workload orchestration

Have build high-scale ML data loaders and preparation systems

Deeply understand every layer of the ML hardware, storage, and networking stacks

Have experience in the NVidia GPU ecosystem

title: Software Engineer: ML Optimization
description:
Software Engineer: ML Optimization

Technical Staff

SFO or BOS

Full-time

On-site

About the Role

We internally call this team MBMB (More Big More Better). You will own optimizations on both the training and on-robot inference stacks. We are still in a regime of step-function, not incremental, gains.

You’ll be responsible for:

Making GPUs go brrrrr

Implementing ML, hardware, and software changes that lead to step-function gains

Optimizing both the inference and training stacks

You might thrive in this role if you:

Are proficient and stay current with the latest ML techniques for training and inference optimizations in transformer and diffusion based architectures

Will chase ML optimizations anywhere: From the CUDA kernels, to ML architecture, to frontend or backend network bottlenecks, CPU bottlenecks, NVLink and comms, to torch, numpy, and Python inefficiencies.

title: Software Engineer: ML Robotics Systems
description:
Software Engineer: ML Robotics Systems

Technical Staff

SFO or BOS

Full-time

On-site

About the Role

You will tackle end to end problems that make our AI models work better on robots. You might add new functionality to our video processing data pipeline, then update our ML data loader, then train some models to validate your change, then test those changes in the real world on a robot. This requires stringing together many distributed python services to accomplish a given data processing, or application processing task. It also requires marshaling large quantities of cloud infrastructure to process this business logic efficiently at scale.

You’ll be responsible for:

Designing and implementing any new idea that can help make our entire system more robust, scalable, or faster

Overhauling existing systems and services to handle the next 10x of scale

Writing the business logic that gets our robot the data it needs, or the business logic that gives our customers the right access to our robots.

You might thrive in this role if you:

Have extensive experience building complex distributed applications or data pipelines at scale

Have experience processing petabytes of data (bonus if it’s video data)

Expertise in python, basic distributed infrastructure skills, and solid modern ML fundamentals

Have a solid foundation in modern ML techniques and experienced large scale ML training and production deployments

Have experience with distributed cloud infrastructure and a solid understanding of cloud networking, permissions, and container orchestration (Kubernetes).

title: Software Engineer: Robotics Controls
description:
Software Engineer: Robotics Controls

Technical Staff

SFO or BOS

Full-time

On-site

About the Role

You will own the software that controls our robots. You will collaborate with ML to turn output intentions into smooth, efficient, safe, and responsive actions on a variety of robot embodiments. You will collaborate with hardware and partners to ensure the system, dynamics, and components are behaving optimally.

You’ll be responsible for:

Designing, implementing, and testing the entire real-time control system from the low-level firmware to the input spec for each embodiment we have

Ownership over a wide variety of robot embodiments from stock industrial co-bot arms to custom hardware

You might thrive in this role if you:

Have extensive experience in rigid body kinematics, and robotic controls

Have experience bringing robots into production environments with stringent reliability requirements

Are experienced in high performance, production-ready Python and C++

Experience with kinematics/simulation libraries

Have experience in robotic middleware platforms such as ROS/ROS2/ZCM
